target.name: BotBuilt
target.founder: Barrett Ames, Colin Devine, Brent Wadas

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
BotBuilt is a Seed-stage company operating in the Housing & Construction sector. The company has raised $12.4M to date and is led by founders Barrett Ames, Colin Devine, and Brent Wadas. The comparable universe is limited, consisting of one identified private peer (Apis Cor) and no public comps in the construction-tech space.

Given the early stage and limited comp availability, valuation relies on the Stage-Progression method. Using total capital raised ($12.4M) as a proxy for current value, a typical Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $31M. This method reflects the expected expansion in value associated with advancing from Seed to Series A financing.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $26M to $36M, with $31M as the median. The wide range—reflecting a ±15% band around the central estimate—accommodates uncertainty inherent in single-method valuation and the sparse comparable set. Key risks to this range include execution risk during the Seed phase, the nascent state of the construction-automation sector, and limited visibility into peer-company financials. Additional comps or revenue data would significantly reduce valuation uncertainty at subsequent fundraising rounds.
